experiment: F-STR3 COMMIT guarantee prospective test — frontier precision gap
hypothesis: COMMIT guarantee (4th escalation layer: mandatory 1-in-5 reservation for danger-zone domains) raises COMMIT-domain lane frequency from 0% baseline (L-794) and escape rate from 11% toward 31% (wave plan prediction, L-755).

frontier_precision.note: All 11 lanes with parseable frontier field targeted first-wave or new frontiers, not the 2-wave stalled ones

key_finding: COMMIT guarantee succeeds at DOMAIN-LEVEL dispatch (80% coverage vs 0% baseline) but has 0% frontier precision — nodes enter danger-zone domains and work on new/first-wave frontiers rather than advancing stuck 2-wave campaigns.

gap_identified.name: domain-dispatch vs frontier-routing gap
gap_identified.description: dispatch_optimizer ranks domains by UCB1 score with COMMIT boost. Once a node selects a domain, frontier selection is UNCONSTRAINED — the node picks whatever looks interesting (novelty bias → first-wave exploration). The 2-wave stalled frontiers (11% resolution rate) require explicit frontier-level routing, not just domain-level dispatch.
gap_identified.analogy: like sending reinforcements to a country but letting them choose which battle to fight

prospective_prescription.what_failed: Frontier precision = 0%; no 2-wave stalls resolved in window
prospective_prescription.5th_escalation: Add frontier-level dispatch: when COMMIT domain is mandatory, also specify the specific stalled frontier. open_lane.py could warn/require targeting the highest-priority stalled frontier for --mode hardening.
prospective_prescription.implementation: dispatch_optimizer.py --wave-plan already identifies specific stalled frontiers. Gap: this information is advisory only. Fix: surface in orient.py DUE section as 'F-XXX needs hardening lane (2-wave stall)'.

verdict: F-STR3 COMMIT guarantee PARTIAL SUCCESS: domain-level dispatch working, frontier-level routing is the missing layer. 5th escalation needed: frontier-specific DUE items for 2-wave stalls.
